{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806","id":2229841201,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6E6K0x","number":3806,"title":"TODO for beta","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6903566946,"node_id":"LA_kwDOCpMuHc8AAAABm3wSYg","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/labels/tracking%20issue","name":"tracking issue","color":"2F4962","default":false,"description":"tracking issue"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-04-07T16:27:24Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T21:49:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"In order to reach beta quality, we need to implement the following items:

* [ ] compile at least 10 codes without any workaround (except the items listed in "after beta" below):
  * [x] [legacy minpack](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/tree/f797ac7721310c7bd98bae416be1bed9975b4203/scipy/optimize/minpack)
  * [x] 60% of SciPy (common blocks and other old features are needed for the rest, we'll tackle after beta)
  * [x] [modern minpack](https://github.com/fortran-lang/minpack)
  * [x] [fastGPT](https://github.com/certik/fastGPT)
  * [x] [dftatom](https://github.com/certik/dftatom) 
  * [x] [stdlib](https://github.com/fortran-lang/stdlib) with small workarounds (due to the issues above)
  * [ ] [lapack](https://github.com/Reference-LAPACK/lapack) #1232
  * [x] [SNAP](https://github.com/lanl/SNAP) #3951 @kmr-srbh, @assem2002
  * [ ] [prima](https://github.com/libprima/prima) #4397
  * [ ] [fpm](https://github.com/fortran-lang/fpm)
* [ ] Other codes to choose from: https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/labels/Code%20to%20be%20Compiled 
* [ ] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/3748: @czgdp1807, @gxyd 
* [ ] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4210
* [ ] All instrinsic functions working (compile time, runtime): @HarshitaKalani, @parth121101
  * [ ] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/492
  * [ ] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4017
  * [ ] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4593
* [ ] robust deallocation (https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3770)
* [ ] most OOP features (@tanay-man)
* [ ] `--fast` working in all cases (not strictly needed, but would be good to have)
* [ ] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2257 @assem2002
* [x] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4236
* [x] robust compile time computation (https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3203): @Pranavchiku, @nikabot 
* [x] https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3581: @Pranavchiku 
* [x] Compiling to WASM via LLVM (few demos, easy to use): @Shaikh-Ubaid 
* [x] Test the emscripten-based LLVM backend at the CI (use nodejs to ensure passing data/arrays from JavaScript to Fortran/WASM works) https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/3954

--------

After we reach beta, we will tackle the following:

* Performance (LLVM already gives us a very good starting point, but to eventually achieve maximum performance that the hardware allows, we need to implement our own optimization ASR->ASR passes for vectorization, inlining, partitioning, etc. and adjust our LLVM backend and its passes)
* Everything working in interactive mode and Jupyter notebook (@Vipul-Cariappa)
* coarrays
* parallel loops:
  * OpenMP: https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3777: @Pranavchiku 
  * GPU offloading to NVIDIA (#4497), AMD (#4535) and other GPUs
  * Using NPUs and other custom hardware from many vendors
* submodules
* full generics support (interface based)
* quadruple precision: https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3468
* good support for lists, dicts and other LPython data structures which we'll support as Fortran extensions (@advikkabra)
* good error messages for all invalid code
* Debug time checks (bounds checks, pointer checks, integer overflow, etc.)
* common block and other F77 features that should not be used in new codes (but we have to support to compile old codes)
* separate compilation (generate intermediate object files, link everything at the end), add robust first class support for `.o` files for all backends: LLVM, WASM, WASM/x64, etc., in all cases (all ASR); to allow good interoperability with other toolchains (so that the user can link this into libraries or codes together with other code)
* Other backends:
  * WASM
  * C/C++
  * NVPTX LLVM backend #4497
  * https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2293
  * https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4319

Also discussed at https://lfortran.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/197339-general/topic/TODO.20for.20Beta.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806/reactions","total_count":10,"+1":5,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":5,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2375324740","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806#issuecomment-2375324740","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3806","id":2375324740,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6NlJRE","user":{"login":"thoth291","id":2971670,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5NzE2NzA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2971670?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291","html_url":"https://github.com/thoth291","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-25T21:49:31Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T21:49:31Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I think that it would be better not to wait for everything to be done to make it work with Jupyter kernel. I can see how it can be useful to have a testing repo with Binder and Colab integration. That would help anyone to test recent version and report back issues if any discovered.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2375324740/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600","id":1636245172,"node_id":"I_kwDOEE6KR85hhx60","number":600,"title":"[bug] juliaup status suggestion differs from what being actually updated","user":{"login":"thoth291","id":2971670,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5NzE2NzA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2971670?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291","html_url":"https://github.com/thoth291","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-03-22T17:54:50Z","updated_at":"2023-05-11T14:30:51Z","closed_at":"2023-05-05T23:21:08Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I installed julia some time ago using juliaup on my PC.
When I did `juliaup list` I've got these versions available.
```
juliaup.exe list
 Channel           Version
--------------------------------------------------
...
 1.8.0~x64         1.8.0+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.0~x86         1.8.0+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8.1             1.8.1+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.1~x64         1.8.1+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.1~x86         1.8.1+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8.2             1.8.2+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.2~x64         1.8.2+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.2~x86         1.8.2+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8.3             1.8.3+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.3~x64         1.8.3+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.3~x86         1.8.3+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8.4             1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.4~x64         1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.4~x86         1.8.4+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8~x64           1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8~x86           1.8.4+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.9               1.9.0-beta2+0.x64.w64.mingw32
...
```
When I checked for what version do I have - I've got:
```
juliaup.exe status
 Default  Channel  Version      Update
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
       *  release  1.8.3+0.x64  Update to 1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32 available
```

Which clearly advised that I can update to 1.8.4 as my next version.
But when I executed `juliaup update` - I've got this:
```
juliaup.exe update
Installing Julia 1.8.5+0.x64.w64.mingw32
```

**My concerns:**
- I didn't see 1.8.5 in the list of available versions - but I see it now, after update
- The update string was misleading - since it mentioned 1.8.4 - but installed a different version.
- The update should start only after additional prompt to confirm that user indeed wants to install that exact version - installation interrupting is the least thing one wants to do

Possible reasons:
- Given that the release of 1.8.5 happened in January (I think) - it's unlikely that release registry for juliaup was updated only now
- Most likely, the reason is in the fact that `juliaup list` and `juliaup status` didn't refresh their local registry and provided me with outdated information.
- However - I did saw 1.8.4 - so that might be speculation on my part.

Hope this will be resolved soon.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/comments/1544093644","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600#issuecomment-1544093644","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600","id":1544093644,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEE6KR85cCP_M","user":{"login":"thoth291","id":2971670,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5NzE2NzA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2971670?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291","html_url":"https://github.com/thoth291","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-11T14:30:50Z","updated_at":"2023-05-11T14:30:50Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@davidanthoff - I just did `juliaup update` and it, without any additional prompt - simply installed julia 1.9 for me.
**This is not good and additional prompt is needed.**
Also - I would like to confirm that I haven't noticed any issues with misleading suggestions vs actual installation since I updated to the last time.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/comments/1544093644/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Ultimately it would be great to use these two modes somehow:

1.  Animated Progress Bar
2.  Stacked Progress Bar

It would be also great to switch programmatically between Stripped and Animated  progress bars - but it might be too much to ask...","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/1038/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/1038/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/comments/1505397349","html_url":"https://github.com/holoviz/panel/issues/1038#issuecomment-1505397349","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/1038","id":1505397349,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCLF6Q85Zuopl","user":{"login":"thoth291","id":2971670,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5NzE2NzA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2971670?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291","html_url":"https://github.com/thoth291","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thoth291/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-04-12T14:39:40Z","updated_at":"2023-04-12T14:39:40Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@philippjfr

```
 1.8.2~x86         1.8.2+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8.3             1.8.3+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.3~x64         1.8.3+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.3~x86         1.8.3+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8.4             1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.4~x64         1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8.4~x86         1.8.4+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.8~x64           1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32
 1.8~x86           1.8.4+0.x86.w64.mingw32
 1.9               1.9.0-beta2+0.x64.w64.mingw32
...
```
When I checked for what version do I have - I've got:
```
juliaup.exe status
 Default  Channel  Version      Update
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
       *  release  1.8.3+0.x64  Update to 1.8.4+0.x64.w64.mingw32 available
```

Which clearly advised that I can update to 1.8.4 as my next version.
But when I executed `juliaup update` - I've got this:
```
juliaup.exe update
Installing Julia 1.8.5+0.x64.w64.mingw32
```

**My concerns:**
- I didn't see 1.8.5 in the list of available versions - but I see it now, after update
- The update string was misleading - since it mentioned 1.8.4 - but installed a different version.
- The update should start only after additional prompt to confirm that user indeed wants to install that exact version - installation interrupting is the least thing one wants to do

Possible reasons:
- Given that the release of 1.8.5 happened in January (I think) - it's unlikely that release registry for juliaup was updated only now
- Most likely, the reason is in the fact that `juliaup list` and `juliaup status` didn't refresh their local registry and provided me with outdated information.
- However - I did saw 1.8.4 - so that might be speculation on my part.

Hope this will be resolved soon.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/600/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

I run the code below
and it failed with this error:
```python
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
/tmp/ipykernel_232494/4169328263.py in <module>
      1 from distributed.diagnostics.progressbar import progress
----> 2 progress(squares,notebook=False,multi=True,complete=True)

/conda/lib/python3.8/site-packages/distributed/diagnostics/progressbar.py in progress(notebook, multi, complete, *futures, **kwargs)
    441         return bar
    442     else:
--> 443         TextProgressBar(futures, complete=complete, **kwargs)

/conda/lib/python3.8/site-packages/distributed/diagnostics/progressbar.py in __init__(self, keys, scheduler, interval, width, loop, complete, start, **kwargs)
    116         **kwargs,
    117     ):
--> 118         super().__init__(keys, scheduler, interval, complete)
    119         self.width = width
    120         self.loop = loop or IOLoop()

/conda/lib/python3.8/site-packages/distributed/diagnostics/progressbar.py in __init__(self, keys, scheduler, interval, complete)
     28 class ProgressBar:
     29     def __init__(self, keys, scheduler=None, interval="100ms", complete=True):
---> 30         self.scheduler = get_scheduler(scheduler)
     31 
     32         self.client = None

/conda/lib/python3.8/site-packages/distributed/diagnostics/progressbar.py in get_scheduler(scheduler)
     22 def get_scheduler(scheduler):
     23     if scheduler is None:
---> 24         return default_client().scheduler.address
     25     return coerce_to_address(scheduler)
     26 

/conda/lib/python3.8/site-packages/distributed/client.py in default_client(c)
   5125         return c
   5126     else:
-> 5127         raise ValueError(
   5128             "No clients found\n"
   5129             "Start a client and point it to the scheduler address\n"

ValueError: No clients found
Start a client and point it to the scheduler address
  from distributed import Client
  client = Client('ip-addr-of-scheduler:8786')
```

**What you expected to happen**:
I expected it to work instead of error.
You can see what I was expecting by modifying `set_as_default=False` to `set_as_default=True` and run it again

**Minimal Complete Verifiable Example**:

```python
from dask.distributed import LocalCluster, Client
cluster = LocalCluster(
     n_workers=5,
     threads_per_worker=1,
     processes=True
)
client = Client(cluster,set_as_default=False) #### <<<<< THIS IS IMPORTANT
import numpy as np
def t(x):
    return x**2
squares = client.map(t, np.arange(100000))
from distributed.diagnostics.progressbar import progress
progress(squares,notebook=False,multi=True,complete=True) #### <<< THIS IS WHERE IT FAILS

results = client.gather(squares) ### this takes surprisingly long time - but I don't know how else I can access the results
print(results[:10])
```

**Anything else we need to know?**:
It used to be working with 2.30.0 version.

**Environment**:

- Dask version:
- dask= 2022.02.0
- distributed= 2022.02.0
- Python version: 3.8.12
- Operating System: CentOS Linux release 7.7.1908 (Core)
- Install method (conda, pip, source): conda

I also did another test and can confirm that it doesn't work in latest: 2022.04.1

**Details**
It's important to me not to make client to be default.
I can make it default for now - to have a workaround - but eventually will have to bring it back to non-default setting.

Would appreciate for any help or advise.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/distributed/issues/6176/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/distributed/issues/6176/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}

```python
     threads_per_worker=1,
     processes=True
)
client = Client(cluster,set_as_default=False) #### <<<<< THIS IS IMPORTANT
import numpy as np
def t(x):
    return x**2
squares = client.map(t, np.arange(100000))
from distributed.diagnostics.progressbar import progress
progress(squares,notebook=False,multi=True,complete=True) #### <<< THIS IS WHERE IT FAILS

results = client.gather(squares) ### this takes surprisingly long time - but I don't know how else I can access the results
print(results[:10])
```

**Anything else we need to know?**:
It used to be working with 2.30.0 version.

**Environment**:

- Dask version:
- dask= 2022.02.0
- distributed= 2022.02.0
- Python version: 3.8.12
- Operating System: CentOS Linux release 7.7.1908 (Core)
- Install method (conda, pip, source): conda

I also did another test and can confirm that it doesn't work in latest: 2022.04.1

**Details**
It's important to me not to make client to be default.
I can make it default for now - to have a workaround - but eventually will have to bring it back to non-default setting.
